title: arctostaphylos pungens subsp. parryana
intro: Arctostaphylos pungens subsp. parryana, also known as Parry's manzanita, is a species of shrub that belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae). This drought-tolerant, evergreen plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of antioxidants and tannins. The health benefits of Parry's manzanita include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and aid in the management of diabetes, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Parry's manzanita can be consumed as a tea, used in topical applications such as salves and creams, or dried to be used in capsules or as a supplement.
